articles.title: Letter: Prediction markets — now the pushback
articles.summary: Critics are mounting organized resistance against the growing influence and legitimacy of prediction markets. The backlash signals that betting markets have gained enough mainstream credibility to threaten established forecasting institutions and media narratives.

articles.title: If one trader can force the outcome of a prediction market, it shouldn’t be tradable
articles.summary: A single trader with enough capital can manipulate prediction markets by forcing outcomes through their actions rather than predicting them, essentially breaking the core mechanism that makes these markets valuable. This exposes a fundamental flaw in how we design prediction markets, since allowing outcome manipulation turns what should be information aggregation into expensive gambling on who has the deepest pockets.

articles.title: Predicting the Scope of Forthcoming Prediction Market Regulation
articles.summary: Regulators are gearing up to set new rules for prediction markets, and the scope of these regulations will determine whether platforms face light oversight or heavy restrictions. The outcome will decide if prediction markets can expand into mainstream finance and politics or get pushed back into niche corners where only crypto degens dare to tread.

articles.title: Prediction markets' latest 'Wild West' accusations: Users are getting suckered by insider trading
articles.summary: Critics are claiming prediction markets have become a free-for-all where insiders with privileged information are cleaning out regular users who don't know they're playing a rigged game. If these accusations stick, they could trigger the kind of regulatory crackdown that would either force platforms to implement costly safeguards or scare away the retail money that keeps these markets liquid.

articles.title: Polymarket’s 5-cent signal was the only thing that got the Netanyahu rumors right
articles.summary: A 5-cent bet on Polymarket correctly predicted Netanyahu-related news that traditional media and other forecasting methods missed entirely. This shows how even tiny-money prediction markets can surface real signal from noise, proving that betting incentives work even when the stakes are minimal.

articles.title: You can bet on climate disasters. Business is booming.
articles.summary: Climate catastrophe bonds and weather derivatives are seeing massive growth as investors pile money into betting on hurricanes, floods, and other natural disasters. The surge signals that prediction markets are expanding beyond politics and sports into literally life-and-death events where information asymmetries and climate data create serious profit opportunities for sharp bettors.

articles.title: Here are the 6 ways lawmakers are trying to tackle the prediction market boom
articles.summary: Lawmakers are scrambling to regulate the exploding prediction markets industry with at least six different legislative approaches currently in the works. The regulatory patchwork could either legitimize prediction markets as mainstream financial tools or strangle them with compliance costs before they reach their potential.

articles.title: Kalshi cofounders' favorite users include 'this guy who lives in Kansas' who is their best inflation forecaster
articles.summary: Kalshi's cofounders discovered their most accurate inflation predictor isn't a Wall Street quant or Fed economist, but some random guy living in Kansas. This highlights how prediction markets can surface genuine forecasting talent from unexpected places, potentially outperforming traditional experts who dominate financial commentary.

articles.title: With casino empire gone, Trump family bets on prediction markets
articles.summary: The Trump family is pivoting from their collapsed casino business to capitalize on the booming prediction markets industry. This signals mainstream legitimacy for political betting and could attract a wave of retail money that follows Trump-branded ventures into previously niche markets.

articles.title: Polymarket opening 'Situation Room' pop-up bar in DC. See when.
articles.summary: Polymarket is launching a temporary "Situation Room" themed bar in Washington DC, capitalizing on election season buzz with what's likely a politically-themed drinking establishment. The pop-up represents Polymarket's push into mainstream culture beyond just crypto degens, using physical presence in the political capital to legitimize prediction markets as serious election infrastructure.

articles.title: ‘A Rigged and Dangerous Product’: The Wildest Week for Prediction Markets Yet
articles.summary: Prediction markets faced their biggest credibility crisis yet as accusations of manipulation and safety concerns dominated headlines across the industry. The fallout could trigger the regulatory crackdown that crypto-style betting platforms have been desperately trying to avoid.

articles.title: Nevada Bans Kalshi for at Least the Next Two Weeks
articles.summary: Nevada regulators have temporarily banned Kalshi, the prediction market platform, from operating in the state for at least the next two weeks. This marks another regulatory crackdown on prediction markets in a key gambling jurisdiction, potentially signaling broader state-level pushback against platforms trying to blur the line between betting and forecasting.
